title: Maltea vacillans
taxonomy: Maltea vacillans is classified as a type of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae, commonly known as the morning glory family. It belongs to the genus Evolvulus. The species name 'vacillans' is Latin for 'vacillating' or 'wavering', which refers to the plant's habit of nodding or swaying in the wind. Maltea vacillans is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. Its classification as an annual or perennial plant can vary depending on the specific growing conditions.
uses: Maltea vacillans is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. It is often used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. The plant's extracts have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. In some cultures, Maltea vacillans is used to treat wounds and promote healing. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal applications.
